data.title: $45M Bitcoin Scandal Triggers Czech No-Confidence Vote
data.description:
The revelation of a significant $45 million Bitcoin scandal has prompted a parliamentary crisis in the Czech Republic, leading to a no-confidence vote against the current government. This scandal has raised serious concerns regarding transparency and accountability within the administration, compelling opposition parties to rally their support for a motion to challenge the ruling coalition's legitimacy and effectiveness. As the political atmosphere intensifies, citizens are closely watching the developments, as the outcome of the vote could have far-reaching implications for the stability and governance of the country.
source: https://voiceofcrypto.online/ampstories/web-stories/45m-bitcoin-scandal-triggers-czech-no-confidence-vote

data.title: Russian authorities bust truck-based crypto mine draining village power
data.description:

data.description:

Ondo Finance has made headlines through its groundbreaking cross-chain settlement with JPMorgan and Chainlink, signaling a new chapter for real-world asset (RWA) integration. Celestia, despite facing short-term.
source: https://crypto-economy.com/qubetics-races-to-sell-out-ondo-hits-jpmorgan-milestone-celestia-slips-which-is-the-most-potential-crypto/

data.title: Hong Kong Sees Growing Interest in Stablecoin Licenses
data.description: According to PANews, Ant International and Ant Digital Technology are planning to apply for stablecoin licenses in Hong Kong, with LianLian Digital also exploring similar opportunities. Xiaomi's subsidiary, Tianxing Bank, has announced a stablecoin partnership with JD Coin Chain Technology, a company under JD.com, which has become one of the initial participants in the Hong Kong Monetary Authority's stablecoin issuer sandbox. The stablecoin market is rapidly expanding, with the global market capitalization reaching approximately $246.382 billion. Internationally, companies like Bank of America and PayPal have announced their stablecoin development efforts, targeting the cross-border payment market. In April 2025, Mastercard introduced a 'stablecoin settlement' option to encourage merchant adoption.

data.title: Discord invite link being used to deliver malware targeting crypto wallets
data.description:
The new malware exploits a weakness in Discord’s invitation system to deliver an information stealer known as Skuld and the AsyncRAT remote access trojan.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/discord-invite-link-malware-crypto-wallets/

data.title: Billionaire Snaps Up $100 Million Of Trump Coin – Details
data.description:
US President Donald Trump’s media group is making a big splash in crypto finance. A Chicago trading firm put up $100 million to buy 4 million shares in Trump Media & Technology Group. That move comes just nine weeks after US regulators closed a probe into the same firm. Based on reports, the deal is tied to TMTG’s plan to buy over $2
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/bitcoin-news/billionaire-snaps-up-100-million-of-trump-coin-after-investigation-ends/

data.title: Binance Market Update (2025-06-14)
data.description:
The global cryptocurrency market cap now stands at $3.26T, down by -3.80% over the last day, according to CoinMarketCap data.

Bitcoin (BTC) has been trading between $104,118 and $106,252 over the past 24 hours. As of 09:30 AM (UTC) today, BTC is trading at $105,011, up by 0.34%.

Most major cryptocurrencies by market cap are trading mixed. Market outperformers include BMT, USTC, and HOME, up by 24%, 18%, and 14%, respectively.
